It seems to me that part of the support for Anthropic and pushback against OpenAI stems from OpenAI's messaging jumbling Ethics, Law, and Norms, leading to confusion and distrust. Anthropic was able to fairly cleanly narrow in on Ethics.

"It was always an inevitability that the government would try to exert control over frontier AI. The problems arise when the government begins exerting control without understanding that the frontier is a living process, not an asset." https://t.co/QnZnosrwen

The only slippery slope here is comms/exec language. No part of the contractual language publicly discussed or clarifications from the OpenAI folks can guarantee NSA/IC will not be able to use their services. OpenAI does not need to "deploy to NSA" for NSA to use those services.

Interesting analysis from Gartner. Given Jevons’s paradox, in the short term (next 5 years) it makes sense that more jobs will be created due to AI than lost. However, the orange line will make a sharp downward turn after 2030s while the blue line will continue its upward trend.
